chapter_context.summary: Micah of Moresheth announces the Lord's coming from His holy temple to judge Samaria and Jerusalem for their transgressions, lamenting the incurable wound of the city and using a series of creative Hebrew wordplays on Judean town names to describe the coming exile and desolation.

outline.text: The Majestic Prophetic Introduction: The Lord Descends to Tread Upon the High Places of the Earth
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Majestic Prophetic Introduction: The Lord Descends to Tread Upon the High Places of the Earth (1:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Micah.

outline.text: Samaria's Destruction and Doom: Laid in Heaps and Her Incurable Wound Reaching the Gates of Jerusalem
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Samaria's Destruction and Doom: Laid in Heaps and Her Incurable Wound Reaching the Gates of Jerusalem (1:5-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Micah.

outline.text: Creative Wordplays and Laments over the Impending Captivity of Judean Cities (Gath, Aphrah, Saphir, Zaanan, Maroth, Lachish, Moresheth-gath, Adullam)
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Creative Wordplays and Laments over the Impending Captivity of Judean Cities (Gath, Aphrah, Saphir, Zaanan, Maroth, Lachish, Moresheth-gath, Adullam) (1:10-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Micah.

key_themes.title: True Worship as Ethical Integrity
key_themes.description: Micah clarifies that religious rituals are meaningless without ethical behavior in everyday commercial, legal, and social interactions.

key_themes.title: Messianic Hope and Bethlehem
key_themes.description: The book introduces the eternal ruler coming from Bethlehem, pointing to the ultimate messianic restoration and security of God's sheep.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and requirements in Chapter 1, what does it practically mean for you to walk humbly with God in your current season of life?
How does the final vision of a compassionate God casting our sins into the deep sea inspire your daily worship and forgiveness of others?

cultural_historical.title: The Southern Kingdom under Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah
cultural_historical.text: Micah's ministry spanned the reigns of three kings of Judah, representing a period of political tension with Assyria, extreme corruption of the elite classes in Jerusalem, and eventually the reforming reign of Hezekiah.
